known_cause: FUNDAMENTAL: AMD climbed ~18% during the week with two stacked catalysts: (1) the Trump administration announced a short-term postponement of U.S. tariffs on Chinese electronics, removing a near-term cost-of-goods headwind on TSMC-fabricated chips; (2) AMD disclosed that Google Cloud's new C4D and H4D virtual machines are powered by 5th Gen AMD EPYC processors, with C4D delivering up to 80% higher throughput/vCPU and H4D optimized for HPC with Cloud RDMA scaling to tens of thousands of cores. Fundamental — both catalysts move forward cash flows: the tariff postponement removes a quantifiable cost headwind, and the Google Cloud C4D/H4D launch converts AMD's 5th Gen EPYC roadmap into deployed hyperscaler instances at one of the three largest cloud providers, expanding contracted server-CPU revenue.
